topics: Kinetics and Reaction Rates: Determination of Kinetics and Reaction Rate of the Identified Reaction - Defining reaction rates for the selected system, performing literature review on temperature and concentration dependency using Arrhenius and Collision theories, collecting experimental data to evaluate activation energy and other kinetic parameters, and preparing a detailed report on reaction kinetics including evaluation of activation energy and determination of rate equations

Reactor Design: Calculating the Volume of the Reactor Required - Classifying and describing types of reactors relevant to the project, developing design equations for single ideal reactors and multiple reactor systems to determine the best system for a given conversion, exploring non-isothermal reactor design by developing the temperature profile, and preparing a reactor design report with detailed calculations and justifications for the chosen reactor configurations

Modeling and Software Analysis: Simulation Studies Using Software - Modeling the selected reaction system and reactors using appropriate chemical engineering software, comparing manual design and calculations with software-generated results, and preparing a final project report that includes all analysis, designs, and software modeling results while highlighting any discrepancies with possible explanations and solutions

Presentation and Submission - Preparing a presentation summarizing the project including key findings, design choices, and comparison between manual and software results, and submitting the final project report and presentation to the faculty
